tested_steps: Reviewed prd.json item infra-004 and confirmed expected scope: S3 uploads, download URLs, and MIME restrictions for image/*, .html, and .pdf.
Ran targeted regression: npx vitest run tests/s3.test.ts, 17/17 tests passed after adding coverage for unsupported content types and HTML charset handling.
Ran full validation: make check passed and make test passed with 35/35 files and 390/390 tests green.
Ran smoke E2E: npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts, 5/5 tests passed.

Ran real AWS S3 verification using the compiled src/lib/s3.ts module: uploaded PDF, HTML, HTML with charset, and PNG objects, fetched each through presigned URLs with HTTP 200, and deleted all test objects successfully.
Verified unsupported MIME handling end-to-end: text/plain upload now rejects with 'contentType must be image/*, text/html, or application/pdf'.

bugs_found.description: uploadFile accepted unsupported MIME types such as text/plain even though the PRD limits support to image/*, text/html, and application/pdf.
bugs_found.expected: Unsupported MIME types should be rejected before the S3 PutObject request is sent.
bugs_found.actual: A real text/plain upload succeeded against S3 before the fix.
bugs_found.reproduction: Call uploadFile({ key: 'qa/sample.txt', body: Buffer.from('plain text'), contentType: 'text/plain' }) and observe that it uploads instead of rejecting.

fixes_applied: Added MIME validation in src/lib/s3.ts to allow only image/*, text/html, and application/pdf, including text/html with charset parameters.
Added regression tests for unsupported MIME rejection and HTML content type with charset in tests/s3.test.ts.

tested_steps: Reviewed prd.json item layout-001 and confirmed the app shell requirements: 10 sidebar nav items, persistent sidebar, active nav highlighting, and route changes across dashboard pages.
Ran automated regression before and after the fix: make test passed with 35/35 files and 392/392 tests green.
Ran Playwright smoke against an isolated webpack dev server on http://localhost:3016 after the fix: tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts passed 5/5.

Ran an Ever CLI manual pass on the isolated server: ever start --url http://localhost:3016/, ever snapshot --mode full to verify all 10 nav items, ever eval to route to /domains and /api-keys, then ever snapshot and ever screenshot to confirm the sidebar persisted and the destination pages rendered.
Captured Ever CLI screenshots during the manual pass to confirm the Emails, Domains, and API Keys dashboard states after navigation.

bugs_found.description: Navigating from the sidebar to /api-keys crashed the page when the database connection failed instead of rendering the API Keys shell with an empty state.
bugs_found.expected: Sidebar navigation should route to /api-keys and keep the app shell usable even if API key data cannot be loaded.
bugs_found.actual: Ever CLI navigation surfaced a server error page with digest 289396507 caused by a Postgres password authentication failure in src/app/(dashboard)/api-keys/page.tsx.
bugs_found.reproduction: Start the local app, open /emails, navigate to /api-keys from the sidebar, and observe that the page throws before rendering the API Keys heading when DATABASE_URL rejects auth.

fixes_applied: Wrapped the API Keys server-page data load in a try/catch and fell back to empty key/domain lists so the page still renders the layout shell and empty state when the database is unavailable.
Strengthened tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts so the sidebar persistence flow now verifies the /api-keys URL and the exact API Keys heading.

tested_steps: Re-ran tests/e2e/templates-list.spec.ts against an isolated QA server using baseURL=http://localhost:3215 so client-side behavior matched normal Next dev hydration.
Cleaned up stale 'E2E Test Template' rows left behind by aborted test runs so the template-card locator matched a single record again.
Confirmed the Templates list shows the created template card, clicking that card navigates to /templates/:id/editor, and the Create template button opens a new editor session.
Re-ran tests/e2e/audience-layout.spec.ts against the isolated QA server with baseURL=http://localhost:3215.

Confirmed the Audience page loads, the 4-tab layout renders, direct navigation between Contacts, Properties, Segments, and Topics works, and the Add contacts trigger reveals Add manually and Import CSV.
Verified the apparent ALL CONTACTS failure was a selector ambiguity because the stat card text and the All contacts combobox option both matched the same Playwright query.
Ran tests/e2e/contacts-list.spec.ts against the isolated QA server with baseURL=http://localhost:3215.
Verified the Audience contacts page renders the search, segment filter, status filter, and export controls.
Independently checked the current dataset on /audience and confirmed the page reaches a stable No contacts found empty state.

bugs_found.description: The stock Playwright test uses an invalid selector string for the contacts empty-state wait.
bugs_found.expected: The test should wait using a valid Playwright locator strategy so QA can distinguish product bugs from test harness bugs.
bugs_found.actual: page.waitForSelector('table, text=No contacts found') fails immediately because Playwright parses it as invalid CSS.
bugs_found.reproduction: Run npx playwright test tests/e2e/contacts-list.spec.ts against the current app; the failing cases abort on the malformed selector before any product assertion runs.

tested_steps: Created an isolated QA worktree ../ralph-to-ralph-3-qa-20260329-171503 on branch qa/20260329-171503-topdown and rebased it onto main before branch-local verification.
Initial isolated Playwright boot on port 3215 failed because the fresh worktree did not have the documented local env vars.
Copying .env from a sibling configured checkout unblocked isolated smoke and deeper feature QA.

bugs_found.description: Fresh isolated QA worktrees do not inherit the documented local env bootstrap, so branch-local dev servers fail until secrets are copied in manually.
bugs_found.expected: A new QA branch/worktree should have a documented, repeatable way to load the required local env vars before running branch-local QA.
bugs_found.actual: The isolated QA worktree started with no DATABASE_URL in shell or local env files. npx next dev --port 3215 initially crashed data-backed pages until .env was copied from another local checkout.
bugs_found.reproduction: Create a fresh worktree from this repo, run npm ci, then start npx next dev --port 3215 before copying any env file. Data-backed pages fail until secrets are bootstrapped manually.

tested_steps: Reviewed prd.json item design-001 and verified the shared DataTable implementation plus current consumers in src/components/logs-list-page.tsx and src/components/api-keys-list.tsx.
Ran automated regression after the fix: make check passed and make test passed with 35/35 files and 393/393 tests green.
Ran Playwright smoke against an isolated webpack dev server on http://localhost:3016 after the fix: tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts passed 5/5.

Ran an Ever CLI manual pass on http://localhost:3016/logs: ever snapshot confirmed the shared table headers and empty state, and ever click on the Method header changed the visible sort indicator and aria-sort state from none to ascending.

Verified row rendering, select-all, independent checkbox selection, actions menu rendering, and sortable-column toggling through unit coverage in tests/data-table.test.tsx because the running app had no seeded table rows available in this environment.

bugs_found.description: The shared DataTable exposed a sortable column contract but ignored it, so sortable headers never rendered interactive controls and the table could not sort rows.
bugs_found.expected: Columns marked sortable should expose an interactive header control and reorder rows when toggled.
bugs_found.actual: The sortable flag was unused, headers rendered as plain text, and row order never changed.
bugs_found.reproduction: Render DataTable with columns that set sortable: true and click the header. Before the fix there is no sort control and the row order does not change.

fixes_applied: Implemented internal sort state in src/components/data-table.tsx with ascending, descending, and reset states plus aria-sort metadata and visible indicators for sortable headers.
Enabled sortable headers on the Logs table so the shared behavior is visible in the running app.
Added regression coverage in tests/data-table.test.tsx for sortable-header toggling and row reordering.
